response.description: The Tabs component is a fundamental UI element designed to organize and navigate content within a limited space on a single page. It acts as a root container that manages the state for a collection of tab triggers and their corresponding content panels. Key features include state management for active tabs, seamless switching between different content sections, and efficient use of screen real estate. The component supports both controlled and uncontrolled usage patterns, allowing for flexible integration into various application architectures. Common use cases include displaying different categories of settings, product details (e.g., description, specifications, reviews), user profiles, or various data views where information needs to be logically grouped and easily accessible without full page reloads. Built on `@radix-ui/react-tabs`, it leverages robust accessibility features and composability, providing a solid foundation for custom tab implementations. The `asChild` prop enables advanced composition, allowing consumers to fully control the rendering of the underlying elements.

guidance.whenNotToUse: Do not use Tabs for navigating to entirely different pages or sections that require a URL change. Avoid using them for content that is unrelated or where all information needs to be visible simultaneously without switching. Alternatives include Accordion for hierarchical content that is less frequently accessed, or a sidebar navigation for primary application navigation.
guidance.whenToUse: Use Tabs when you need to group related content into distinct sections and allow users to switch between them within the same view, optimizing screen space and providing clear navigation. Ideal for displaying different views of the same object (e.g., user profile data, product specifications, settings categories) or organizing complex forms into logical steps.
guidance.accessibility: The Tabs component follows WAI-ARIA authoring practices. It ensures proper keyboard navigation (arrow keys for tab switching, Tab key for content focus), `aria-selected` state management, and semantic roles like `tablist`, `tab`, and `tabpanel`. Ensure visible focus indicators are present for keyboard users. Content within tab panels should also be accessible.

common.description: Demonstrates how to control the active tab state externally using `value` and `onValueChange` props.
common.title: Controlled Tabs

common.description: Shows how to disable a specific tab trigger, preventing user interaction.
common.title: Tabs with Disabled Trigger
common.code:
import React from 'react'; import * as Tabs from '@radix-ui/react-tabs'; const DisabledTabs = () => ( Active Tab Disabled Tab Another Tab

This tab is active.

You should not see this content directly by clicking the tab, only if navigated via keyboard or initial state.

Content for another active tab.

 ); export default DisabledTabs;

common.description: Utilizes the `asChild` prop to render a custom button as the tab trigger, maintaining Radix UI's accessibility and behavior.
common.code:
import React from 'react'; import * as Tabs from '@radix-ui/react-tabs'; const CustomTriggerTabs = () => (

Welcome to your Dashboard!

View your key metrics here.

Generate Reports

Access detailed analytics and reports.

 ); export default CustomTriggerTabs;
common.title: Tabs with Custom Trigger using `asChild`
